text: DeepSeek recently released DeepSeek-R1, an open-source model that diverges significantly from OpenAI’s ChatGPT, offering a powerful alternative for the AI community. This paper examines the architecture of DeepSeek-R1, which distinguishes itself by primarily utilizing rule-based reinforcement learning (RL) and bypassing the traditional preliminary supervised fine-tuning (SFT) stage for increased efficiency. By integrating a multi-stage training process—including "cold-start" data before the RL phase—the model demonstrates exceptional performance in complex reasoning tasks, further optimized by a precise reward modeling system. Our analysis shows that DeepSeek excels in technical and logical domains, offering transparency through its open-source framework, whereas ChatGPT remains the leader in general-purpose tasks, creative content generation, and conversational fluency. Despite the breakthroughs represented by both models, the AI landscape still faces critical hurdles, including data quality, the "black box" nature of neural networks, privacy concerns, and the socioeconomic impact of job displacement. This paper explores the future of the field, anticipating advancements in multimodal capabilities and more efficient handling of massive datasets to improve the utility and accessibility of artificial intelligence for all users.
